Marcelo Ceron

About

Second-year Electrical Engineering student with a strong interest in embedded systems, digital systems, and power engineering. I enjoy applying engineering fundamentals to real-world problems through hands-on projects — from microcontroller programming to hardware-software integration.

Embedded C Java MATLAB Microcontrollers Digital Systems Power Engineering Hardware Integration
Projects

KL43Z Interrupts & SysTick Embedded Systems

2025

Implemented interrupt-driven and timer-based control on the NXP KL43Z microcontroller using Embedded C, deepening understanding of real-time embedded systems.

  • Used GPIO interrupts to trigger Interrupt Service Routines (ISR)
  • Configured SysTick timer for precise 0.25-second intervals
  • Implemented conditional LED control logic via hardware registers
Embedded C NXP KL43Z GPIO SysTick

Automated Plant Watering System

2024

Designed an automated irrigation system combining Java control logic and MATLAB analysis to monitor soil moisture and control water delivery in real time.

  • Integrated soil moisture sensors for continuous monitoring
  • Developed Java logic to activate water pump on threshold events
  • Used MATLAB to analyze and optimize sensor data
  • Calibrated timing delays and safety constraints to prevent overwatering
Java MATLAB Sensors Control Systems

YorkU CNCC – Concrete Canoe Team

2024 – 2025

Contributed to designing a lightweight, structurally optimized concrete mix for intercollegiate competition.

  • Reduced mix density while maintaining structural integrity
  • Performed compressive strength testing across multiple batches
  • Optimized material ratios for competition performance
